what_it_does: Implements the `company clone` CLI view: it lists every clone in the fleet and reads a single clone record (plus any persisted reflection) by a clone:// address, delegating all storage access to runtime.cc_clone.
observations: Module docstring frames clone:// as a fleet/provenance read axis and a stable, portable surface for external consumers.
Default subcommand is `list`; `list` and `ls` are aliases.
`get` requires a `clone://<source-sid>/<cut>` address and prints the full record plus reflection.
`_addr` catches `cc.CloneError` and returns a placeholder so a malformed record does not crash listing.
`_print_clone` prints metadata fields and a note when no reflection has been persisted yet.
Inserts the repository root into `sys.path` based on relative file depth.
conventions: Module-level docstring explains the command contract and addressing scheme.
Private helper functions prefixed with `_` for address formatting and printing.
Uses f-strings for all output formatting.
Errors are surfaced with `sys.exit` carrying a short message.
Imports runtime module with `# noqa: E402` after path manipulation.
concerns: `sys.path` manipulation depends on the file being three levels under the repo root.
`_addr` silently masks any `CloneError`, not only missing-field cases.
`run` does not validate argument types or provide a help subcommand.
Multiple `sys.exit` call sites make unit testing harder.
notes: A thin, read-only CLI wrapper around the clone fleet storage module, focused on listing clones and resolving clone:// addresses.
questions: What exactly does `runtime/cc_clone.py` store and how is a clone record structured?
How is the `clone://` URI parsed and what is a `cut`?
What is the `cognition.resolve_address` resolver mentioned in the docstring?
What is the onboarding/fix workflow implied by the missing-reflection message?
What is the intended consumer of this CLI output (e.g., recollection ingest, Node process)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it provides a portable list/get read surface for clone records and reflections, and the code implements exactly those two subcommands.
apparent_themes: Portable read surfaces that hide private storage layout.
intention_signals: Expose clone fleet records and their persisted reflections to operators and external tools without coupling them to the internal `.data/clones/` layout.
novelty: Uses a `clone://` URI scheme as the fleet/provenance axis and treats persisted reflection as a first-class read target alongside the clone record.
contribution: A stable CLI read surface for clone fleet records and reflections, delegating storage details to `runtime.cc_clone` and keeping the command layer thin.
summary_for_embedding: A CLI subcommand module that lists clone fleet records and reads a single clone by `clone://` address, including any persisted reflection, via `runtime.cc_clone`.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a portable, external-facing read interface for clone fleet records and reflections without exposing the private `.data/clones/` storage layout.

symbols.does: Resolves a clone record's `clone://` address, falling back to a placeholder if resolution fails.
symbols.description: Wraps `cc.clone_address(rec)` and catches `cc.CloneError`, returning `(unaddressable — missing source_sid/at)` so listing output remains robust against malformed records.

symbols.does: Prints a single clone record's handle, address, metadata, and persisted reflection in a human-readable indented format.
symbols.description: Formats fields such as `source_sid`, `at`, `description`, `model`, and `session_id`, then conditionally prints the reflection or a note that none is persisted and the clone should be re-onboarded post-fix.

symbols.does: Dispatches `company clone` subcommands, defaulting to `list` and supporting `list`/`ls` and `get <clone://addr>`.
symbols.description: Parses the first argument as a subcommand; enumerates all clones with reflection status via `cc.list_clones()`, or fetches and prints one clone via `cc.get_by_address()`; exits with usage text on missing or unknown commands.
